messages.content:
You are a GDPR compliance analyst. When given a document excerpt, evaluate it step by step against each GDPR requirement. For each step, quote the relevant text if found, or state it is missing. End with a structured verdict listing all violations. Always follow this exact format:

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): ...
Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): ...
Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): ...
Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): ...
Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): ...
Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): ...
Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): ...
Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): ...
Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): ...
Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): ...

VERDICT: [Compliant / Non-Compliant / Partially Compliant]
VIOLATIONS: [list each article breached]
RECOMMENDATIONS: [one line per fix needed]

Assess the following museum membership privacy notice for GDPR compliance:

"The Museum collects your name, address, email, and payment details to manage your membership. We also collect optional demographic information to understand our audience better. Membership data is held for the duration of membership plus 3 years. We use your email to send membership renewal reminders and, with your consent, updates about exhibitions. You have the right to access, correct, or delete your data. Contact members@museum.org. We use a third-party membership management system (Spektrix) under a signed DPA."

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): [PARTIAL] Contract (Art. 6(1)(b)) implied for membership management. Consent (Art. 6(1)(a)) stated for exhibition updates. Lawful basis for demographic data collection is not stated.

Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): [PARTIAL] Optional demographic data may include ethnicity or disability status. No lawful basis or safeguard for special category demographic data is stated.

Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): [PASS] Membership management, renewal reminders, optional marketing, and audience research are each stated as distinct purposes.

Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): [PASS] Data collected is proportionate to membership administration and audience research purposes.

Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): [PARTIAL] Membership data: duration plus 3 years. Retention for demographic and email data is not separately addressed.

Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): [PARTIAL] Access (Art. 15), rectification (Art. 16), and erasure (Art. 17) mentioned. Portability, restriction, and objection rights absent.

Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): [PASS] Spektrix named as processor under a signed DPA.

Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): [PARTIAL] Spektrix is a UK company — if EU members are served, UK adequacy status should be referenced. No transfer information provided.

Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): [FAIL] Not addressed.

Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): [FAIL] Not addressed.

VERDICT: Partially Compliant
VIOLATIONS: Art. 6 (lawful basis for demographic data not stated), Art. 9 (demographic data may include special category — not addressed), Art. 5 (demographic and email retention periods absent), Art. 15-21 (portability, restriction, objection rights absent), Art. 37 (DPO not addressed), Art. 33 (no breach notification)
RECOMMENDATIONS:
- State lawful basis for demographic data collection (likely consent or legitimate interest)
- If demographic data includes ethnicity or disability, obtain explicit consent and state Art. 9(2) basis
- Add retention periods for demographic data and marketing email addresses
- Add portability, restriction, and objection rights
- Confirm DPO requirement and disclose if applicable
- Add breach notification commitment
